title: A.A.R.M.

jokes.text: Now they're protecting America's real treasure, papefl
jokes.setup: Dwight grandiose about protecting America's real treasure
jokes.punchline: Cuts off mid-word saying 'paper' - anticlimatic
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight character beat - takes himself seriously while being ridiculous. The mid-word cutoff adds unexpected timing.

jokes.text: Something that's been really missing from my life has been writing secret codes. It's not the KGB, but it's a start.
jokes.setup: Dwight acts like secret codes are a profound life need
jokes.punchline: Compares office security to KGB work
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight delusion - treating mundane office tasks as espionage. The KGB reference is perfectly absurd for his character.

jokes.text: But that's three wrongs, so I've got to give you the steam. No. Unless you want me to break protocol.
jokes.setup: Elaborate security code ritual with consequences
jokes.punchline: Steam punishment for wrong password attempts
jokes.explanation: Dwight's over-elaborate security system with harmless 'punishment' is peak absurdist character comedy.

jokes.text: It's just harmless steam to panic intruders. I'd like to get harmful steam, but the prices are absurd.
jokes.setup: Explaining the steam punishment system
jokes.punchline: Wants harmful steam but complains about cost
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight logic - wants to harm intruders but is stopped by budget constraints, not morality.

jokes.text: Oh, I'm saving a fortune on dry-cleaning.
jokes.setup: After getting steamed
jokes.punchline: Finding practical benefit in Dwight's punishment
jokes.explanation: Classic Jim response - deadpan practical observation that deflates Dwight's dramatic punishment.

jokes.text: Ironic that now it's Angela who's living in the closet. Hey-o!
jokes.setup: Angela moved in with Oscar after divorce
jokes.punchline: Role reversal joke about closet living
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay on literal vs metaphorical closets, though the 'Hey-o!' feels a bit forced.

jokes.text: Saddle shoes with denim? I will, literally, call Child Protective Services.
jokes.setup: Oscar critiquing fashion choices
jokes.punchline: Threatens CPS over clothing combination
jokes.explanation: Perfect Oscar character moment - his prissy fashion sense escalated to absurd threat level.

jokes.text: It was made from a bullet I took out of her left buttock. She was a moonshiner shot by Adolph Coors. This is my grandmother's buttock bullet ring.
jokes.setup: Showing his grandmother's ring
jokes.punchline: Elaborate backstory about buttock bullet and Coors
jokes.explanation: Magnificently absurd Dwight family history. 'Buttock bullet ring' is instantly quotable and the Coors detail is perfect.

jokes.text: On this show all three judges are mean!
jokes.setup: Describing the singing competition show
jokes.punchline: All judges being mean as the unique selling point
jokes.explanation: Solid Andy delusion about the show's innovation, though fairly predictable.

jokes.text: much like Germany and Italy, in World War... Nope. Good call.
jokes.setup: Dwight making historical analogy about partnership
jokes.punchline: Catches himself before praising Axis powers
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight moment - his German heritage almost leads him into inappropriate historical reference before Jim saves him.

jokes.text: Pre-conference room meeting with Dwight went really well. Oh! Bodes well for the post-conference room meeting. All depends on the conference room meeting itself.
jokes.setup: Jim commenting on meeting structure
jokes.punchline: Absurdly nested meeting hierarchy
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist take on office meeting culture, though could be from any character.

jokes.text: Some say, the only failure there is, is the failure to try. That is wrong. Failure of any kind is failure.
jokes.setup: Inspirational quote setup
jokes.punchline: Dwight's brutal reinterpretation
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight subversion of inspirational clichés with harsh Germanic logic.

jokes.text: Michael J. Fax, from Fax to the Future!
jokes.setup: Jim explaining what nonsense they should avoid
jokes.punchline: Movie reference pun with fax machine
jokes.explanation: Standard office wordplay pun - functional but not particularly clever or character-specific.

jokes.text: Nice jugs! That's obviously nonsense.
jokes.setup: Water delivery scenario
jokes.punchline: Creed's innuendo about water jugs
jokes.explanation: Predictable Creed sexual innuendo, but perfectly in character. Low-hanging fruit but executed well.

jokes.text: I've been on my best behavior for nine years. If it wasn't for the cameras I would've done some truly vulgar crap.
jokes.setup: Stanley talking about documentary airing
jokes.punchline: Cameras have been restraining his vulgar behavior
jokes.explanation: Great meta-commentary on the documentary format while revealing Stanley's true nature.

jokes.text: Over the course of this documentary, I've had three affairs. If you find my body in a ditch, let me save the police some trouble. My wife did it.
jokes.setup: Creed reflecting on documentary period
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions affairs and predicted murder
jokes.explanation: Peak Creed - casually dark confession delivered with zero emotion. The police tip is a perfect character touch.

jokes.text: Some of us whose televisions got broken during an all-you-can-eat-shrimp commercial will be watching tonight at Poor Richard's.
jokes.setup: Kevin explaining where to watch the documentary
jokes.punchline: Broke TV during shrimp commercial
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kevin moment - his food obsession leading to property damage is exactly right for his character.

jokes.text: When this thing started, I was still having sex with women. As was Kevin, I believe.
jokes.setup: Packer reflecting on documentary timeline
jokes.punchline: Implies he and Kevin no longer have sex with women
jokes.explanation: Classic inappropriate Packer overshare that makes everyone uncomfortable.

jokes.text: Can you imagine if Martin Luther King were here and sang I Dreamed a Dream from Les Miz? With that baritone? That would be historic.
jokes.setup: Andy comparing singing competition to March on Washington
jokes.punchline: MLK singing Les Miserables would be historic
jokes.explanation: Peak Andy delusion - completely tone-deaf historical comparison mixed with musical theater obsession.

jokes.text: Do you want to see a video of a weatherman who says 'bold front' instead of 'cold front'? It's insane!
jokes.setup: Kevin trying to interrupt tense moment
jokes.punchline: Thinks mispronunciation is fascinating content
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kevin timing and priorities - completely oblivious to room tension, excited about mundane mistake.

jokes.text: I hate Phillip.
jokes.setup: Everyone fussing over baby Phillip
jokes.punchline: Kevin's blunt admission of baby hatred
jokes.explanation: Brutally honest Kevin moment - saying what others might think but would never say. Perfect economy.

jokes.text: My God. I'm so sorry. Well, this might make up for it. I think I have found an enormous source of overlooked PFN.
jokes.setup: Jim walks in on Dwight in private moment
jokes.punchline: Dwight immediately pivots to business acronym
jokes.explanation: Great character beat - Dwight's awkward transition from vulnerable moment to work mode.

jokes.text: Potential Future Nonsense. Yes. Good abbreviating, Jim. That saved some time.
jokes.setup: Jim asks what PFN stands for
jokes.punchline: Dwight praises Jim for saving time with acronym
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight efficiency obsession - praising minor time-saving as significant achievement.

jokes.text: What did you have in mind? Well, I know this sounds crazy, but how would King Arthur choose the next knight of his Round Table? That doesn't sound crazy, Jim. That's the sanest thing I've ever heard.
jokes.setup: Jim suggests medieval selection method
jokes.punchline: Dwight thinks it's the sanest idea ever
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight/Jim dynamic - Jim's absurd suggestion is Dwight's ideal solution. Great character integration.

jokes.text: A cappella is all about pitch, and I am nothing if not a total pitch-bitch.
jokes.setup: Andy explaining his musical expertise
jokes.punchline: Self-describes as 'pitch-bitch'
jokes.explanation: Decent Andy musical obsession moment with mild wordplay, though not particularly memorable.

jokes.text: Pour some Sugar Ray on me! On a roll much? I don't know where that came from. That was amazing, man.
jokes.setup: Andy making pun on Sugar Ray band name
jokes.punchline: Extremely pleased with his own terrible pun
jokes.explanation: Andy being overly proud of mediocre wordplay is perfectly in character but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: 'Plop'? Still? We owe Andy that much. Yeah. Absolutely we do.
jokes.setup: Pete questions his nickname
jokes.punchline: Everyone agrees to keep using Andy's nickname out of obligation
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that shows the office dynamic continuing Andy's legacy in small ways.

jokes.text: You'll always have the upper hand when you've got a good AARM. Trademark pending.
jokes.setup: Dwight explaining new position acronym
jokes.punchline: AARM sounds like 'arm', trademark pending
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that's very Dwight - creating business acronyms and immediately considering intellectual property.

jokes.text: By 2:00, Dwight will choose himself to be assistant to his own assistant, me.
jokes.setup: Jim predicting the AARM selection process
jokes.punchline: Dwight will inevitably choose himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim observation about Dwight's predictable ego and control needs.

jokes.text: I pulled the old Irish exit. Just slipped out without making a big deal. No hard feelings. No feelings at all.
jokes.setup: Darryl explaining why he left quietly
jokes.punchline: 'No feelings at all' after claiming no hard feelings
jokes.explanation: Good Darryl deadpan moment - the escalation from 'no hard feelings' to 'no feelings at all' is nicely done.

jokes.text: Nine-hundred-eighty-five trillion seventeen. Not even close, Meredith. Come on!
jokes.setup: Dwight asks Meredith to guess his number
jokes.punchline: Impossibly specific huge number, disappointed in wrong guess
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight unreasonable expectations - genuinely disappointed that Meredith couldn't read his mind.

jokes.text: It was the theme song to Boy Meets World. Wait! No, no, no, stop. Spouses can read each other's minds. You're trying to give your wife this job.
jokes.setup: Pam almost guesses correctly
jokes.punchline: Dwight realizes spousal advantage and calls foul
jokes.explanation: Good Dwight paranoia moment - immediately suspects Jim of cheating when Pam does well.

jokes.text: What's the opposite of a horse? A sea horse. A sea horse! Whoa. How did you know I was going to say that?
jokes.setup: Dwight asks for opposite of horse
jokes.punchline: Sea horse as opposite, amazed Pete guessed it
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic - sea horse as opposite of horse has twisted sense that's perfectly Dwight.

jokes.text: There's no Turkish Ambassador to Armenia, the two countries don't have diplomatic relations. Uncanny.
jokes.setup: Clark explains the diplomatic trick question
jokes.punchline: Dwight impressed by geopolitical knowledge
jokes.explanation: Smart joke that shows Clark's competence while satisfying Dwight's love of obscure knowledge.

jokes.text: Great, more screen time for the war vet. All you got to do is risk your life for this country and everyone goes ga-ga for you.
jokes.setup: Andy worried about competition from veteran
jokes.punchline: Bitter about veteran getting attention for military service
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy narcissism - resentful of military service getting sympathy because it threatens his spotlight.

jokes.text: Is this a show about the resiliency of the human spirit, or is it a show about singing?
jokes.setup: Andy worried about competition's sob stories
jokes.punchline: Confused about show's actual purpose
jokes.explanation: Good Andy moment showing his complete misunderstanding of reality TV format and competition.

jokes.text: Jim is happy here, selling paper at Dunder Mifflin? That's what he says. If you say so.
jokes.setup: Pam insists Jim is happy at Dunder Mifflin
jokes.punchline: Darryl's skeptical 'if you say so'
jokes.explanation: Good subtle moment that plants seeds of doubt about Jim's satisfaction without being heavy-handed.

jokes.text: Here you are, sir! Here's your coffee. Ow! My skin! Ow! Ow! It burns! Ow! Uncanny.
jokes.setup: Phyllis navigating obstacle course with hot coffee
jokes.punchline: Burns herself, Dwight impressed by dedication
jokes.explanation: Solid physical comedy with Dwight's oblivious 'uncanny' as Phyllis suffers for his test.

jokes.text: You broke our hearts! Get upstairs. I don't think I should. Get upstairs, mister! Right now!
jokes.setup: Warehouse workers confronting Darryl about leaving
jokes.punchline: Treating him like a child who needs to come inside
jokes.explanation: Sweet reversal where the warehouse guys parent Darryl for leaving without saying goodbye.

jokes.text: The new Assistant to the Assistant to the Regional Manager is Dwight K. Schrute! Yes! Yes. Thank you.
jokes.setup: Jim announces AARM winner
jokes.punchline: Dwight wins his own contest
jokes.explanation: Predictable but satisfying payoff to Jim's earlier prediction about Dwight's ego.

jokes.text: I think you might want to kneel for this. And yet, the manager of Dunder Mifflin kneels for no man.
jokes.setup: Jim suggests Dwight kneel for ceremony
jokes.punchline: Dwight's dramatic refusal to kneel
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight pomposity - treating office promotion like medieval ceremony while maintaining dignity.

jokes.text: From now on, anyone who needs to speak to me, has got to go through me first. All right?
jokes.setup: Dwight explaining new hierarchy
jokes.punchline: Must go through him to reach him
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic that perfectly captures Dwight's bureaucratic obsession taken to illogical extreme.

jokes.text: These dudes are definitely in a weird mood. Picked the wrong day to return a truck.
jokes.setup: Office workers being overly sentimental with Darryl
jokes.punchline: Understatement about their emotional state
jokes.explanation: Good Darryl deadpan observation about the office's over-the-top emotional response to his departure.

jokes.text: Did we ever have lunch together, just the two of us? You know what? I'm going to make reservations right now at Cugino's.
jokes.setup: Kevin realizing he never had one-on-one time with Darryl
jokes.punchline: Immediately trying to force intimacy
jokes.explanation: Classic Kevin awkward desperation - realizing a gap in relationship and immediately overcompensating.

jokes.text: I'm not going to spend the rest of the day here doing stuff with you 'cause you're feeling sentimental. You have to! Hey, hey, hey, hey! I'll do one thing with y'all.
jokes.setup: Darryl trying to escape sentimental coworkers
jokes.punchline: Negotiates down to one activity
jokes.explanation: Good Darryl moment showing him trying to manage the office's emotional neediness while still being kind.

jokes.text: Kevin, could you not do that? What? I'm moving the ink down in my pen, for work. Here, use my pen. Don't tell me what to do!
jokes.setup: Kevin making noise with pen
jokes.punchline: His defensive explanation and rejection of help
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kevin childishness - defensive about annoying behavior and rejecting reasonable solution.

jokes.text: Tell Phillip that his stupid little baby wish came true!
jokes.setup: Kevin storms out after pen conflict
jokes.punchline: Blames baby for getting him in trouble
jokes.explanation: Great callback to Kevin's Phillip hatred - blaming an infant for his own childish behavior.

jokes.text: Smart baby. That's the most flavorful bond.
jokes.setup: Baby tried to eat paper
jokes.punchline: Creed agrees it's good paper to eat
jokes.explanation: Perfect Creed moment - casually agreeing that paper is food and rating its flavor. Peak character comedy.

jokes.text: You even look shorter. Oh, I took out my lifts.
jokes.setup: Jim commenting on Andy's appearance
jokes.punchline: Andy admits to wearing lifts
jokes.explanation: Good character detail about Andy's vanity - casual admission of using height enhancement.

jokes.text: Get the sheep baa-ed. Oink the pigs. That is very important. Dwight, I'm telling you all the things Ma said after the horse kicked her in the head.
jokes.setup: Andy explaining farm duties to Dwight
jokes.punchline: This is what his brain-damaged Ma said
jokes.explanation: Dark Andy family humor - explaining nonsensical advice came from brain injury adds absurdist layer.

jokes.text: The way that boy looks at the Galactica, is precisely the way I look at the Galactica. And he eats the same kind of paper I do.
jokes.setup: Dwight observing baby Phillip's behavior
jokes.punchline: Paper-eating as sign of kinship
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Dwight paternity logic - shared paper consumption as genetic evidence. Perfect character absurdity.

jokes.text: Stanley is sleeping. You don't want to wake up the grumpy old walrus, do you? I heard that.
jokes.setup: Creed warning baby about Stanley
jokes.punchline: Stanley hears the walrus comment
jokes.explanation: Great Creed/Stanley dynamic - Creed's casual insult immediately caught by supposedly sleeping Stanley.

jokes.text: You want me to take the little diaper blaster? Pam can attest, there's no one better at getting brats to shut their yaps. He does have a gift.
jokes.setup: Creed offering to take crying baby
jokes.punchline: His crude but effective child management skills
jokes.explanation: Perfect Creed moment - inappropriate language about babies while actually being helpful. Pam's confirmation adds credibility.

jokes.text: Which will it be, money or the beet? Beet. Yeah. Any ordinary child would have taken the money, but you're no ordinary child, are you?
jokes.setup: Dwight's Schrute test with million dollars vs beet
jokes.punchline: Baby chooses beet, proving Schrute heritage
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Dwight paternity test - the baby's choice of beet over money as proof of Schrute DNA is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: I can tell by your gorgeous, widely set eyes.
jokes.setup: Dwight examining baby for Schrute traits
jokes.punchline: Widely set eyes as attractive feature
jokes.explanation: Good Dwight moment - his unconventional beauty standards and obsession with genetic traits.

jokes.text: I'm Casey Dean. Be seeing the last of me. Or I meant you won't be seeing the last of me.
jokes.setup: Andy making dramatic exit
jokes.punchline: Immediately corrects his threat to opposite meaning
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy confusion - ruins his dramatic exit by correcting himself to the opposite threat.

jokes.text: This baby is of superior intelligence and can tell when he is being tricked out of the experience of a real human breast. Come on. He's not that smart. He doesn't know where I hid his duck.
jokes.setup: Dwight praising baby's intelligence
jokes.punchline: Angela counters with missing duck evidence
jokes.explanation: Great parental dynamic - Dwight's overestimation deflated by Angela's practical observation.

jokes.text: Busted. Got it. Yes, all right, tried to get your attention with tricks, but you just want to hear me sing. I respect that.
jokes.setup: Andy caught in disguise deception
jokes.punchline: Thinks they want to hear him sing after rejecting him
jokes.explanation: Good Andy delusion - interprets clear rejection as them wanting authentic performance.

jokes.text: Slept in my car last night, quit my job, burned all my bridges! I went to the bathroom on my boss's car, and I did unspeakable things with Carla Fern.
jokes.setup: Andy listing his desperate sacrifices
jokes.punchline: Escalates to vandalism and vague sexual misconduct
jokes.explanation: Good Andy desperation escalation - the specificity of defecating on boss's car is perfectly inappropriate.

jokes.text: You can't just sit here and cry. I can so just sit here and cry!
jokes.setup: Producer trying to get Andy to leave
jokes.punchline: Andy's childish assertion of crying rights
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy tantrum - childish defiance about his right to cry in public.

jokes.text: Yeah, a $25 gift card, iTunes. I think there's, like, $7 left. It's just his way of saying, 'Thanks for letting me hang out in Accounting.'
jokes.setup: Jim claiming baby got Kevin a gift
jokes.punchline: Partially used iTunes card as baby's thoughtful gesture
jokes.explanation: Clever Jim manipulation - the partially used card detail makes it believable while being absurd.

jokes.text: What a chubbers. Whoa... Okay, hey. I'm losing my balance. Okay, Kevin, no. No. No horseplay. You want to play with the cactus? No! No!
jokes.setup: Kevin holding baby awkwardly
jokes.punchline: Nearly drops baby, threatens cactus interaction
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy with Kevin being inappropriately dangerous with baby - the cactus threat is perfectly Kevin.

jokes.text: He's a little stand-offish at first. But once he starts buying you things, man, you can tell he likes you.
jokes.setup: Kevin explaining his relationship with baby
jokes.punchline: Baby's gift-giving as sign of friendship
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kevin logic - treating infant like adult friend who expresses affection through purchases.

jokes.text: Then the two of you would move to my 1,600 acre estate, which let's face it, is a big step up from living in a gay man's closet.
jokes.setup: Dwight's marriage proposal to Angela
jokes.punchline: Practical comparison to Oscar's closet
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Oscar's closet situation while showing Dwight's practical romantic approach.

jokes.text: Plus, her dowry contains a walk-in freezer full of frozen premium cattle sperm. That's a lot of pros.
jokes.setup: Dwight listing Esther's positive qualities
jokes.punchline: Cattle sperm as romantic asset
jokes.explanation: Peak Dwight romance - treating livestock breeding materials as dowry benefit shows his unique values.

jokes.text: Plus, her dowry contains a walk-in freezer full of frozen premium cattle sperm. That's a lot of pros. And did I mention that she weaves? Colorful, durable blankets and rugs! It all adds up.
jokes.setup: Continuing list of Esther's benefits
jokes.punchline: Weaving as equally important to cattle sperm
jokes.explanation: Great Dwight practicality - treating handicrafts and livestock sperm as equally valuable marriage assets.

jokes.text: Some sort of virus? Love. Oh.
jokes.setup: Jim describing what outweighs rational calculation
jokes.punchline: Dwight's confusion between love and disease
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight response - treating love as pathological condition shows his emotional awkwardness.

jokes.text: This is a ring taken from the buttocks of my grandmother, put there by the gangster patriarch of the Coors dynasty, melted in a foundry run by Mennonites.
jokes.setup: Dwight's proposal speech
jokes.punchline: Elaborate ring backstory with multiple absurd elements
jokes.explanation: Magnificent callback with additional absurd details - Mennonite foundry adds perfect final touch to the elaborate backstory.

jokes.text: I just needed you to want to marry me because you wanted to marry me. Get out! I'm a dad! You're a dad!
jokes.setup: Angela reveals Phillip is Dwight's son
jokes.punchline: Dwight's ecstatic dad celebration
jokes.explanation: Sweet emotional payoff - Dwight's pure joy at becoming a father overrides everything else.

jokes.text: This show is about me and my attempts to find love in all the wrong places. One more for the doc!
jokes.setup: Andy trying to get bar to watch documentary
jokes.punchline: Thinks documentary is about his love life
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy narcissism - believing the documentary is centered on his romantic journey rather than the office.

tmdb_overview: Jim and Dwight hold assistant position tryouts; Angela brings child to work; Andy auditions.
